exceptions.subject: Where to Look navigation table
exceptions.reason: The Where-to-Look table is the canonical navigation index per CLAUDE.md § Where to Look — pointers to incident archaeology, CHANGELOG, release script, and version anchor. The table format is intentionally retained inline so the user can scan the four cross-reference targets without opening another file. Moving this to claudedocs/ would defeat the table's role as the in-CLAUDE.md routing index.

exceptions.subject: Release Process runbook section
exceptions.reason: The Release Process section is a process runbook the SP follows on every push (CLAUDE.md § Release Process). The schema-shape detection fires because the runbook contains step-by-step substructure with tables for bump classification and file changes. Moving the runbook to claudedocs/ would split the SP's authoritative push procedure across two files. Accepted as intentional inline content.

exceptions.subject: SemVer bump classification table
exceptions.reason: Sub-step of the Release Process runbook (see exception above). The table maps change types to bump levels — load-bearing for the manual review the SP performs on every release. Sub-section of an accepted parent exception.

exceptions.subject: Release-bump file-change table
exceptions.reason: Sub-step of the Release Process runbook (see exception above). The table specifies which three files change at which lines for every release. Sub-section of an accepted parent exception.

exceptions.subject: ${CLAUDE_*} glob mentioned in Project Facts
exceptions.reason: The string `${CLAUDE_*}` appears in CLAUDE.md § Project Facts as a glob illustrating the family of env vars hooks must NOT use (per the Provisional Guard 'Don't use `${CLAUDE_*}` env vars in hook commands'). The literal `CLAUDE_*` glob is by design unimplemented anywhere — that is the rule's point. The S3 candidate-extraction slugifies the `CLAUDE_*` token to 'claude', so the fingerprint anchors on that normalized form. Intentional.

exceptions.subject: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} cited in Provisional Guard scope
exceptions.reason: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} is named in the Provisional Guard scope list as one of the phantom env vars hooks must NOT rely on (per CHANGELOG v5.4.0 → v5.4.1 archaeology). The variable is intentionally not implemented anywhere in SP — the Guard documents that absence. The `claudedocs/INCIDENTS.md` reference and reference doc mentions of CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR are documentation, not implementation. Intentional.
